input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges="None"` is correct for the page, but there are a few other things that commonly cause leftover gaps:

**1. Hide the navigation bar** — if your page is inside a `Shell` or `NavigationPage`, their chrome still reserves space:

```xml
<!-- Shell -->
<ContentPage Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             Shell.TabBarIsVisible="False" ...>

<!-- or NavigationPage -->
<ContentPage NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False" ...>
```

**2. Zero out page padding** — some styles/templates set default padding:

```xml
<ContentPage Padding="0" ...>
```

**3. Make the Image actually fill the space** — use `Fill` layout options and an appropriate `Aspect`:

```xml
<Image Source="photo.jpg"
       Aspect="AspectFill"
       HorizontalOptions="Fill"
       VerticalOptions="Fill" />
```

**Putting it all together:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"
             Shell.TabBarIsVisible="False"
             SafeAreaEdges="None"
             Padding="0">

    <Image Source="photo.jpg"
           Aspect="AspectFill"
           HorizontalOptions="Fill"
           VerticalOptions="Fill" />
</ContentPage>
```

The most common culprit is the navigation bar still being visible — `SafeAreaEdges` only controls the system bar insets, not the app's own navigation chrome.
data.reasoningText:
The user is working on a .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10 and wants an image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar and navigation bar. They've set `SafeAreaEdges="None"` on the ContentPage but still see padding.

This is a common .NET MAUI question about edge-to-edge/full-screen layouts. Let me think about what they might be missing:

In .NET MAUI, to achieve edge-to-edge content:

1. `SafeAreaEdges` - Controls whether content respects safe area insets. Setting to `None` should allow content to go behind system bars.


2. On Android specifically, .NET 9 introduced edge-to-edge rendering by default, but there are additional considerations beyond just the `SafeAreaEdges` property that might be preventing the image from truly filling the screen.

Individual views like the Image control can use `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` to extend beyond safe areas. If the page is wrapped in a `NavigationPage` or `Shell`, the navigation bar takes up space and needs to be hidden with `NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False"` or `Shell.NavBarIsVisible="False"`. The ContentPage itself might also have default padding that needs to be zeroed out.

On iOS, the safe area handling works differently and requires careful use of `IgnoreSafeArea` or proper `SafeAreaEdges` configuration. Let me think through this more carefully for .NET MAUI specifically.

In newer versions, `SafeAreaEdges` provides more granular control than the older `IgnoreSafeArea` property. Setting `SafeAreaEdges="None"` should prevent safe area padding, but if the user still sees padding, it's likely coming from elsewhere. The most common culprits are a navigation bar from a parent `NavigationPage` or `Shell`, default padding on the page itself, or margins on the image. On Android, achieving true edge-to-edge display might require additional configuration beyond just the XAML properties.
